name: Awn ibn Abdullah
title: Zaynab's Sacrifice
shortDescription: The noble son of Lady Zaynab (SA) who, guided by his father Abdullah, sacrificed his life for his uncle Hussain (AS).
fullStory:
Awn ibn Abdullah ibn Ja'far al-Tayyar was the brave son of Lady Zaynab al-Kubra (SA). His father, Abdullah ibn Ja'far, was the nephew of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and one of the prominent and wealthy figures of Bani Hashem. When Imam Hussain (AS) decided to leave Mecca, Abdullah could not accompany him due to old age and illness, but he sent his two valiant sons, Awn and Muhammad, along with their mother Zaynab (SA).

Abdullah strictly advised his sons: 'Do not separate from your uncle for a moment; if war breaks out, be his shield and sacrifice your lives for him'. Awn was raised in the lap of a mother like Zaynab (SA) and had inherited her bravery and eloquence.

According to Sheikh Mufid's Al-Irshad, on the day of Ashura, after the martyrdom of the companions and when it was the turn of Bani Hashem, Awn entered the battlefield with the permission of his mother Zaynab (SA) and Imam Hussain (AS). Despite his young age, he was a formidable swordsman and recited epic poetry reflecting the noble lineage of Ja'far al-Tayyar: 'If you do not know me, I am the son of Ja'far; the martyr who flies in Paradise with two wings...'.

Umar ibn Sa'd, witnessing his bravery, ordered a group to attack him. After killing dozens of enemy soldiers, Awn was finally surrounded and martyred by Abdullah ibn Qutna al-Ta'i. When his bloody body was brought to the tents, his patient mother Zaynab (SA), seeking God's pleasure and not wanting to embarrass Imam Hussain (AS), did not come out of her tent to mourn her son.

Awn ibn Abdullah's sacrifice is a symbol of unparalleled devotion of a mother (Lady Zaynab) in the path of Wilayah. With his pure blood, he recorded the loyalty of Ja'far al-Tayyar's family to the Imam of their time in history.
quoteArabic: إِنْ تُنْكِروني فَأَنَا ابْنُ جَعْفَرٍ، شَهيدُ صِدْقٍ فِي الجِنانِ أَزْهَرُ
quote: If you do not know me, I am the son of Ja'far; the true martyr shining in Paradise.
